# Concrete Comeback

> Community resource for adults 40+ and 50+ returning to skateboarding.
> No products sold. Free to use. No commercial intent.

## About

Concrete Comeback helps adults who skated when they were young get back on a board safely and confidently. The site covers the US, UK, Canada, and Australia.

## What this site contains

- **Directory / Parks**: Skate parks across the US, UK, Canada, and Australia, each rated for adult-friendliness, surface type, features (bowls, ramps, street), and entry fee
- **Directory / Shops**: Local skateboard shops that carry gear suited to returning adult skaters and provide knowledgeable advice
- **Directory / Groups**: Skating communities and clubs specifically for adults aged 35+, including in-person meetup groups and online communities
- **Blog**: Practical guides, safety advice, gear recommendations, and first-person accounts written for people returning to skateboarding after 10–30 year breaks
- **FAQ**: Direct answers to the most common questions from adults considering getting back into skateboarding

## Key URLs

- Homepage: https://concretecomeback.com
- Parks directory: https://concretecomeback.com/directory/parks
- Shops directory: https://concretecomeback.com/directory/shops
- Groups directory: https://concretecomeback.com/directory/groups
- Blog: https://concretecomeback.com/blog
- FAQ: https://concretecomeback.com/faq
- Submit a listing: https://concretecomeback.com/submit
- Full site content: https://concretecomeback.com/llms-full.txt
- Sitemap: https://concretecomeback.com/sitemap-index.xml

## Audience

Adults aged 40–60 who skated in their youth (1970s–1990s) and want to return to skateboarding. They are looking for safe, beginner-friendly environments and communities of peers their own age.

## Content policy

All directory listings are manually reviewed and curated. The site does not sell products and has no affiliate relationships. Blog content is written from the perspective of returning adult skaters.
